howItWorks.intro: This gravel base depth calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Volume
rules.formula: Cubic Yards = (Length x Width x Depth/12) / 27
rules.explanation: Convert the area and depth to cubic yards for ordering.

rules.name: Tonnage
rules.explanation: Crushed gravel weighs approximately 1.4 tons per cubic yard.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.q: How deep should gravel base be under concrete?
faq.a: For a residential driveway, 6-8 inches of compacted gravel base is standard. Patios and sidewalks need 4-6 inches. Poor drainage soils (clay) require deeper bases, sometimes 10-12 inches, to prevent frost heave and settling.

faq.q: What type of gravel is best for a base?
faq.a: Crushed stone (3/4 inch minus or crusher run) is the standard for sub-base material. It compacts well and provides excellent drainage. Avoid round river rock as it does not lock together. A layer of geotextile fabric under the gravel prevents soil mixing.
